title: Binghamia olowinskiana

The exact classification of Binghamia olowinskiana is not well-documented in botanical sources.

intro: Binghamia olowinskiana, commonly known as Bingham's tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Binghamia olowinskiana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
